description_long: This is a large, elongated barn with a steeply pitched gable roof, featuring a symmetrical design and prominent wooden trusses at each end. The exterior is clad in closely spaced horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and traditional appearance. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground, supported by a series of vertical posts along the perimeter, which may provide ventilation or storage space beneath. The roof extends well beyond the walls, creating deep eaves that offer protection from the elements. There are no visible windows or doors on the long sides, and the ends are solid except for the central truss detail. The barn sits on a rectangular patch of grass, suggesting it is freestanding and not attached to other buildings.
description: A large, traditional wooden barn with a steep gable roof, horizontal plank siding, and deep overhanging eaves, supported by perimeter posts. The structure is rustic and symmetrical, with no visible windows or doors on the sides.
description_view_0: End view showing the steep gable roof, central wooden truss, and overhanging eaves; no doors or windows visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long, horizontal plank siding, evenly spaced support posts, and the full length of the barn; no openings present.
description_view_2: Opposite end view mirroring view 0, with the same gable roof and truss detail; no visible entry points.
description_view_3: Side view identical to view 1, showing the extended roof, horizontal siding, and support posts along the base.
attribute_door_style: single large central door implied at the gable end, style not clearly visible
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
